content.problemStatement:
The bustling town of Siruseri has just one sports stadium. There are a number of schools, colleges, sports associations, etc. that use this stadium as the venue for their sports events.

Anyone interested in using the stadium has to apply to the Manager of the stadium indicating both the starting date (a positive integer SSS) and the length of the sporting event in days (a positive integer DDD) they plan to organise. Since these requests could overlap it may not be possible to satisfy everyone. Also, there should be at least one gap day between any two approved events, so that the stadium can be cleaned.

It is the job of the Manager to decide who gets to use the stadium and who does not. The Manager, being a genial man, would like to keep as many organisations happy as possible and hence would like to allocate the stadium so that maximum number of events are held.

Suppose, for example, the Manager receives the following 4 requests:

 

`      Event No.   Starting Date        Length 
       
       1                   2                    5
       2                   9                    7
       3                  15                    6
       4                   9                    3
` 

He would allot the stadium to events 111, 444 and 333. Event 111 begins on day 222 and ends on day 666, event 444 begins on day 999 and ends on day 111111 and event 333 begins on day 151515 and ends on day 202020. You can verify that it is not possible to schedule all the 444 events (since events 222 and 333 overlap and only one of them can get to use the stadium).

Your task is to help the manager find the best possible allotment (i.e., the maximum number of events that can use the stadium).

Input:The first line of the input will contain a single integer NNN indicating the number of events for which the Manager has received a request. Lines 2,3,...,N+12,3,...,N+12,3,...,N+1 describe the requirements of the NNN events. Line i+1i+1i+1 contains two integer SiS_iSi​ and DiD_iDi​ indicating the starting date and the duration of event iii.

Output:Your output must consist of a single line containing a single integer MMM, indicating the maximum possible number of events that can use the stadium.
constraints: 50%50 \%50% of test cases will also satisfy 1≤N≤100001 \leq N \leq 100001≤N≤10000.
